title: Antipater of Sidon
content: Antipater of Sidon (Greek: Ἀντίπατρος ὁ Σιδώνιος, "Antipatros ho Sidonios") was an ancient Greek poet of the second half of the 2nd century BC. Cicero mentions him living at Rome in the time of Crassus and Catullus, and calls him a brilliant epigram mist, sometimes too fond of imitation. His poems preserved in the "Greek Anthology" include evocations of art and literature and epitaphs, but there appears to be confusion in the "Anthology" between Antipater of Sidon and Antipater of Thessalonica, who lived in the following century. Antipater composed an epitaph for Sappho, in which he stated that she died of natural causes and was buried in her homeland.
